summary: Iteration 2 testing of COVENANT: RECURSION (FPS prototype). Backend pytest: 5/5 passed. Frontend e2e: all 13 specified UI flows passed (menu render, DEPLOY SOLO -> operator-select, 5 operators visible with details panels, DEPLOY -> game view with R6 control bindings WASD/Q-E/C/SHIFT/RMB-ADS/R/F/1-2-3/SCROLL, BACK TO MENU from game, BACK from operator-select, SQUAD DEPLOY -> lobby with player-name-input + create-room-btn + room creation, SYSTEMS -> settings with all 3 tabs). Zero console errors during full e2e run.

minor.issue: Accepts arbitrary dict - no Pydantic LeaderboardUpdate model; non-blocking but recommended for input validation
Orphan rooms created via REST (without WebSocket connect) are never garbage collected - rooms dict can grow unbounded over time

issues: Three.js console deprecation warnings noted in iteration 1 (THREE.Clock, PCFSoftShadowMap) - non-blocking. Did not re-observe during pre-pointer-lock screen this run since canvas init is gated on click.

action_items: Optional: Add Pydantic model for /api/leaderboard POST payload validation (player_name required, kills/deaths int).
Optional: Add periodic cleanup for empty rooms in `rooms` dict (orphans created via REST POST never get pruned).
Optional: Replace deprecated Three.js APIs (Clock -> Timer, PCFSoftShadowMap -> PCFShadowMap).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: Two near-identical game_loop usage paths (multiplayer vs singleplayer WebSocket handlers). The singleplayer handler duplicates ~40 lines of logic from the multiplayer one - candidate for refactor into shared helper.
server.py: rooms dict has no expiry/cleanup for REST-created rooms - memory leak vector.

OperatorSelect.jsx: data-testid ids are 'operator-medic' and 'operator-hacker' but display names are PATCH and CIPHER - inconsistent. Test request acknowledges this; document the mapping clearly to avoid confusion.
OperatorSelect.jsx: detail panel uses hovered state preferentially over selected - if user selects then hovers another, the selection visually appears to switch in the panel. Minor UX consideration.
GameView pre-lock screen 'BACK TO MENU' button has no explicit data-testid. Test worked via text match, but adding data-testid='game-back-btn' is recommended.
Frontend uses absolute hosted image URL in MainMenu (customer-assets.emergentagent.com). If that asset becomes unavailable, the menu degrades gracefully (falls back to dark gradient) - acceptable.
seed_data_creation: Leaderboard entry 'TEST_Commander' from iteration 1 still present (idempotent upsert). New room created via lobby UI with player name 'TEST_T1' (in-memory only).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 13 review-request items pass. Test_ids verified: main-menu, menu-singleplayer, menu-multiplayer, menu-settings, operator-select, operator-{vanguard|sentinel|phantom|medic|hacker}, operator-deploy-btn (disabled until selection), operator-back-btn, lobby-screen, player-name-input, create-room-btn, lobby-back-btn, settings-panel, settings-tab-{controls|audio|video}, settings-back-btn. GameView pre-lock screen shows CLICK TO DEPLOY + full R6 control legend (WASD, SHIFT-Sprint, C-Crouch, Q/E-Lean L/R, LMB-Shoot, RMB-ADS, R-Reload, F-Ability, 1/2/3 or SCROLL-Switch weapon) and BACK TO MENU button (no data-testid - selected by text). Three.js pointer-lock gameplay (movement, weapon switching, ability F-key, round system) requires real mouse and is not automatable. Backend WebSocket endpoints /ws/game/{room_id} and /ws/singleplayer not exercised via automation but REST surface fully tested.
rca of the issue: No issues found - all required tests pass.
